reasoning: The base rate for NATO Article 5 invocation is exceptionally low, having occurred only once in the organization's 75-year history. As of the knowledge cutoff on November 9, 2025, it has not been invoked during the 2025 window. With only 52 days remaining in the year, the likelihood of a major triggering event is extremely remote. While there has been an uptick in hybrid warfare, airspace violations, and sabotage in late 2024 and 2025 (which led to an Article 4 invocation in September 2025), these fall well short of the unanimous 32-member consensus required for Article 5. Experts suggest Russia will not be prepared for a conventional conflict with NATO until at least 2029, and U.S. military statements in October 2025 noted that Russia appeared "deterred." Given this, the probability is capped by the lowest allowable bound for this prediction setting (0.05).
